one_line_takeaway: Swapping DreamerV3's pixel-reconstruction encoder for a frozen pretrained V-JEPA-2 encoder yields a far more data-efficient world model (5k vs 100k training steps) with lower collision rates on highway/roundabout HighwayEnv scenarios, but it underperforms DreamerV3 on reward across most tasks and fails badly (0.970 collision rate) on lane-merging, a failure the authors themselves attribute to possible reward hacking.
key_finding: Results are mixed, not a clean win for HanoiWorld. Collision rate (lower is better): HanoiWorld achieves 0.200 +/- 0.400 on highway-v0 (vs DreamerV3 0.550 +/- 0.497, VQ-VAE 1.000 +/- 0.000) and 0.340 +/- 0.473 on roundabout-v0 (vs DreamerV3 0.500 +/- 0.500, VQ-VAE 0.570 +/- 0.495) -- best of the three. But on merge-v0, HanoiWorld collides in 0.970 +/- 0.170 of episodes -- far worse than DreamerV3's 0.030 +/- 0.171 and worse than VQ-VAE's 0.290 +/- 0.454. Average reward (higher is better): DreamerV3 dominates on highway-v0 (51.065 +/- 52.700 vs HanoiWorld's 13.163 +/- 23.277) and merge-v0 (41.973 +/- 2.896 vs HanoiWorld's 13.480 +/- 5.703); HanoiWorld only roughly matches DreamerV3 on roundabout-v0 (9.818 +/- 6.252 vs 9.423 +/- 6.171). The authors themselves note the merge-v0 result (near-total collision rate of 0.970 co-occurring with a moderate reward of 13.480) is suspicious and hypothesize reward hacking -- the agent may be exploiting the reward function by favoring collision-inducing behavior that still yields consistent reward. They also note the model favors overly conservative 'safe-lane' selection in roundabout-v0, suggesting the entropy regularization for exploration is underperforming.
core_contribution: HanoiWorld is a JEPA-based world model for autonomous-vehicle control that replaces DreamerV3's pixel-reconstruction encoder with a frozen, pretrained V-JEPA-2 visual encoder. A downstream bottleneck MLP projects V-JEPA-2's high-dimensional features into a compact 1024x128 latent, jointly optimized with a parallel 2D-CNN spatial-prediction branch using an L1 alignment loss plus VICReg variance/covariance regularization (weights alpha=1.0, beta=1.0, gamma=0.1). These learned embeddings feed a DreamerV3-style RSSM (deterministic state h_t via RNN + stochastic state z_t, reward predictor, Bernoulli continuation predictor) and a simple MLP actor-critic trained via REINFORCE with lambda-returns on imagined rollouts (imagination horizon H, discount gamma=0.997, entropy coefficient beta=3e-4). The encoder is finetuned on a 2D bird's-eye-view (BEV) image dataset rendered from nuScenes LiDAR point clouds; the RSSM and actor-critic are trained inside the HighwayEnv simulator. The authors' stated novelty is architectural composition (frozen JEPA encoder + RSSM + lightweight actor-critic), not the RSSM, actor-critic algorithm, or VICReg loss themselves -- those are explicitly adopted from DreamerV3 (Hafner et al.) and VICReg (Bardes et al.), respectively.
threat_model: N/A -- not a security paper. This is a reinforcement-learning / world-model paper for autonomous-vehicle planning; it contains no adversary, attacker capability, or security threat model of any kind. Note the paper does use the term 'safety-awareness' but only in the RL/control sense (collision avoidance), not an AI-security sense.
limitations: Explicitly acknowledged by the authors: (1) merge-v0 failure with suspected reward hacking -- near-total collision rate despite non-trivial reward, unexplained by ablation; (2) entropy-based exploration regularization appears too conservative, biasing the agent toward safe-lane selection rather than flexible navigation in roundabout-v0; (3) no integration of global/contextual conditioning (e.g., language-conditioned encoders, occupancy/graph representations of the whole environment) that could improve generalization; (4) no evaluation of imagination-horizon or imagination-temperature ablations, unlike prior world-model work they cite (Ha & Schmidhuber); (5) the paper does not evaluate at scale beyond three HighwayEnv scenarios with low-fidelity 64x64 RGB observations, and does not test on real-world driving data despite the encoder being nuScenes-finetuned; (6) despite improved collision rate on two of three scenarios, the paper's own reward metric shows HanoiWorld underperforms the DreamerV3 SOTA baseline on two of three tasks, so the overall planning quality is not established as superior, only data-efficiency (5k vs 100k training steps) is a clear advantage.

datasets_used: HighwayEnv simulator scenarios: highway-v0, merge-v0, roundabout-v0 (evaluation)
nuScenes dataset (LiDAR point clouds rendered to 2D BEV RGB images, used to finetune the V-JEPA-2-based encoder)
quantitative_results: Collision rate highway-v0: HanoiWorld 0.200+/-0.400, DreamerV3 0.550+/-0.497, VQ-VAE 1.000+/-0.000
Collision rate merge-v0: HanoiWorld 0.970+/-0.170, DreamerV3 0.030+/-0.171, VQ-VAE 0.290+/-0.454
Collision rate roundabout-v0: HanoiWorld 0.340+/-0.473, DreamerV3 0.500+/-0.500, VQ-VAE 0.570+/-0.495
Average reward highway-v0: HanoiWorld 13.163+/-23.277, DreamerV3 51.065+/-52.700, VQ-VAE 3.121+/-12.047
Average reward merge-v0: HanoiWorld 13.480+/-5.703, DreamerV3 41.973+/-2.896, VQ-VAE 30.114+/-11.664
Average reward roundabout-v0: HanoiWorld 9.818+/-6.252, DreamerV3 9.423+/-6.171, VQ-VAE 3.826+/-4.643
Training steps: DreamerV3 and VQ-VAE trained 100,000 steps; HanoiWorld trained 5,000 steps (RSSM/actor-critic) atop a frozen pretrained V-JEPA-2 encoder
Encoder latent size: 1024 x 128 bottleneck projection
Hyperparameters: discount gamma=0.997, EMA teacher momentum tau=0.996, encoder loss weights (alpha,beta,gamma)=(1.0,1.0,0.1), entropy coefficient beta=3e-4, VICReg epsilon=1e-4
baselines_compared: DreamerV3 (Hafner et al. 2023) -- RSSM-based world model, current SOTA long-horizon planner
VQ-VAE + ConvLSTM (Robine et al. 2020) -- small discrete latent world model, originally for Atari with PPO
